summary.intent: Add a Nostr DM channel to Moltis, bringing it to parity with existing channels (Telegram, Discord, Slack, MS Teams, WhatsApp, Matrix) with NIP-04/NIP-44 encrypted DMs, relay management, OTP access control, web UI, docs, and CI integration.
summary.outcome: Fully implemented the moltis-nostr crate with plugin lifecycle, config, inbound bus, outbound, access control, seen-event dedup, NIP-01 profile publishing, metrics instrumentation, NIP-44 fallback decryption, integration tests, web UI add/edit/onboarding flows, documentation, and E2E Playwright tests across 6 commits; PR #674 created, review comments resolved, merged on top of main.

repo: ChannelType enum is #[non_exhaustive] so new variants don't require updating every match arm — wildcard arms in gateway/channel.rs and chat/lib.rs handle unknowns automatically
ChannelsConfig uses #[serde(flatten)] for an `extra` map; new channels should be added as named fields (matching telegram/discord/slack/msteams/whatsapp pattern) rather than going through extra
Feature flag chain: crates/cli (default features) → crates/httpd → crates/gateway (optional dep + feature) → crates/nostr; same pattern as matrix/whatsapp
Contract tests in crates/channels/src/contract.rs are designed for TestPlugin and require an empty {} config to start — not suitable for real channel plugins that need valid credentials
ALL constant in plugin.rs has a companion test asserting the count matches the enum discriminant count — must be updated when adding variants
moltis_channels::message_log::MessageLog is the correct import path; ChannelOtpProvider is re-exported at moltis_channels root
nostr-sdk 0.44 nip04 and nip44 features are NOT in default features — must be explicitly enabled in workspace Cargo.toml
nostr-sdk prelude imports nip44::{self, *} which brings nip44 functions into scope directly, causing ambiguity with nip04::decrypt — use fully qualified ::nostr_sdk::nips::nip44::decrypt
nostr-sdk Client::notifications() returns a broadcast::Receiver that only sees messages sent AFTER creation — must subscribe before connect() to avoid missing EOSE/events
nostr-sdk subscribe() takes a single Filter, not Vec<Filter>; disconnect() returns () not Result
Metadata::picture() takes a Url type, not String — must parse with Url::parse()
taplo fmt reorders TOML keys alphabetically — will touch unrelated Cargo.toml files (msteams, secret-store, webhooks)
biome formatter requires chained method calls to be split across lines when they exceed line length — .split().map().filter() pattern on one line fails fmt check
The website/index.*.html files (all 10 locales) already had Nostr references pre-added — only the SVG icon was missing
The worktree uses unrelated git histories between helix-epoch and main (main is a single squashed commit) — merge requires cherry-pick strategy, not git merge/rebase

code.finding: ALL constant needs updating alongside the enum; companion test asserts count == discriminant count
UTF-8 safe truncation: use str::floor_char_boundary(MAX_MESSAGE_BYTES) instead of manual while loop or naive slice
SharedConfig = Arc<RwLock<NostrAccountConfig>> must be shared between AccountState and the bus loop to avoid config drift on live updates
update_account_config must detect [REDACTED] sentinel in secret_key field and preserve existing key to prevent corruption
RwLockReadGuard must be dropped before any .await point — extract dm_policy and other values into local variables first
named_fields() method and Default impl must stay in sync with struct fields; adding a named channel field without updating named_fields() breaks schema validation
biome requires chained .split().map().filter() to be broken across multiple lines
New channels need to be added to both ChannelTypeSelector options AND the ChannelStep form routing AND channelDisplayLabel()

workflow: nostr-sdk API discovery requires grepping the vendored source in ~/.cargo/registry — cargo doc is faster but not always available offline
When nostr-sdk prelude glob causes ambiguity with std types (time, Instant), use explicit crate-qualified paths rather than fighting the glob
Integration tests for network-dependent features should create the broadcast::Receiver before calling connect() to avoid race conditions
taplo fmt should be run after cargo fmt to avoid spurious TOML diffs in CI

When a worktree branch has unrelated history from origin/main (single squash commit), use git log to identify the Nostr-specific commits then cherry-pick onto a fresh branch from main rather than merging

friction: nostr-sdk v0.44 nip04/nip44 features not in defaults — silent compile failure until feature flags were added
nostr-sdk prelude glob imports nip44 symbols directly causing ambiguity with nip04 — required fully qualified paths
nostr-sdk Client::notifications() broadcast receiver misses events if created after connect() — took multiple test failures to diagnose the timing issue
The worktree had an unrelated git history vs origin/main (single squash commit) making git merge produce 70+ conflicts — required cherry-pick strategy
biome formatter line-length rules for chained method calls not caught during development, only surfaced in CI
Metadata::picture() type mismatch (Url vs String) only caught at compile time after writing profile.rs
OtpState::new() signature mismatch only caught at compile time — required reading Telegram's otp.rs for the correct call
open_items: NIP-17 gift-wrapped DMs (more private, less widely supported) — explicitly deferred as future enhancement
NIP-65 relay list advertisement — deferred as future enhancement
Media attachments via NIP-94 — deferred as future enhancement
Reactions via NIP-25 — deferred as future enhancement
Metrics feature currently activates moltis-nostr even when nostr feature is off in non-default builds — dep?/feature syntax added but should be verified in minimal-feature CI builds
NOSTR_TEST_BOT_KEY and NOSTR_TEST_SENDER_KEY secrets need to be added to GitHub CI secrets manually (gh secret set commands provided but not executed)
Integration test for DM round-trip depends on relay propagation timing (30s timeout) — may be flaky on congested relays
Seen-event tracker (seen.rs) debounced persistence to nostr_seen.json is implemented but the data_dir() path binding to the gateway's configured data directory was not verified end-to-end
